properties.remark: California Highway Patrol reported a mud/dirt/rock slide along Highway 36 near mile marker 60 from heavy rain.

Between 5:18 PM and 5:21 PM PST a tornado was reported by a spotter and appeared on area webcams and radar. Tehama County Sheriff and Office of Emergency Services have not reported any damage thus far. The tornado occurred in an unpopulated area. This report is preliminary and details will be confirmed on Saturday January 4th with an official determination released via Public Information Statement and social media channels.

Corrects previous tornado report from 8 NE Dairyville. Between approximately 5:18 pm and 5:21 pm local time on Friday a trained spotter and area webcam footage showed a brief tornado about 8 miles northeast of Dairyville in an unpopulated area of Tehama County. All evidence including radar was viewed and evaluated in coordination with the Tehama County Sheriff and Office of Emergency Services. There have been no damage or injury reports. There will not be a damage survey team sent to the area as there is no damage to survey. The preliminary determination is that this was an EF-U (unknown) tornado that touched down for approximately 3 minutes with a path length of approximately 0.8 miles and unknown peak wind.

There were around 100 almond trees uprooted during a thunderstorm on March 17, 2025. It has been determined from a review of radar and photos of the damage that the event was due to straight line winds, estimated at 70 mph.
Public reporting 1 inch hail in Kirkwood at the school. Additionally, CHP reporting hail covering nearby Interstate 5.

Brief EF-0 tornado touched down at 2 ENE Salida at approximately 2:13 PM PDT and was on the ground for 0.9 miles until 2:15 PM PDT. The tornado moved through several orchards and 3 properties. Dozens of almond trees were uprooted and large tree branches were broken. Minor structure damage observed at the properties included a covered patio and roof material ripped off, a broken window, an outbuilding and metal fencing was displaced, and a large trailer was lifted and moved approximately 20 feet. Estimated max wind of 76 mph. Estimated path width of 70 yards. NWS damage survey conducted in partnership with Stanislaus County Office of Emergency Services.
